CheckTitle: Conditional Access policies have Continuous Access Evaluation enabled
Description: Microsoft Entra **Conditional Access** evaluates whether at least one enabled policy targeting all users and all apps has **Continuous Access Evaluation (CAE)** configured in its session controls. CAE allows Microsoft Entra ID to revoke access in near real-time when critical events occur (user account disabled, password change, admin-initiated revocation, IP-based location policy changes) rather than waiting for token expiry.
Risk: Without CAE enabled, there is a window of time (up to 1 hour by default for access tokens) where a compromised or disabled account can still access resources. This gap enables **token replay**, **session hijacking**, and **privilege escalation** attacks after **credential theft** or **account compromise**.

Code.Other:
1. Go to Microsoft Entra admin center (https://entra.microsoft.com/)
2. Navigate to Protection > Conditional Access > Policies > New policy
3. Users > Include > Select users and groups > Select All users
4. Target resources (Cloud apps): Select All cloud apps
5. Session > Enable Continuous Access Evaluation
6. Enable policy: On, then Create

Recommendation.Text: Enable **Continuous Access Evaluation** in Conditional Access policies that target all users and all apps. CAE allows near real-time token revocation during critical events like account disable, password change, or location-based policy changes. This significantly reduces the window of opportunity for attackers to use compromised tokens.
